sense.definition: a small-scale version of golf with fun obstacles

examples.x: We played miniature golf at the beach.
The miniature golf course had a windmill.
Miniature golf is perfect for date nights.
She loves miniature golf on weekends.
He scored a hole-in-one in miniature golf.

sense.definition: the last nine holes of an 18-hole golf course

examples.x: During our game today, I played better on the back nine than the front nine.
Sarah's strong drive on the back nine helped her clinch the victory.
The back nine of this course offers stunning views of the lake.
Mark's concentration wavered on the back nine, affecting his performance.
On the back nine, the wind picked up, making it challenging to gauge the distance.

sense.definition: the initial nine holes of a golf course, typically numbered one through nine

examples.x: Golfers often focus on their strategy during the front nine to set a good score for the entire round.
Playing the front nine requires precision and strategy to navigate through various hazards and challenges.
Many golfers find the front nine to be an enjoyable and challenging part of their round.
Golfers may experience different weather conditions and course features as they progress through the front nine.

sense.definition: a golf hole that bends to the left or right

examples.x: The first hole is a challenging dogleg to the right.
He drove the ball perfectly around the dogleg.
She struggled with the sharp dogleg on the seventh hole.
A dogleg left can be difficult for right-handed golfers.
They designed the course with many doglegs to add variety.

sense.definition: a golf scoring format where the total number of strokes is counted

examples.x: They competed in a stroke play tournament.
She prefers stroke play over match play.
His best score in stroke play is 72.
Stroke play requires consistent performance.
The championship was decided by stroke play.

sense.definition: a short golf shot to get the ball onto the green

examples.x: He used a chip shot to get close to the hole.
Her chip shot landed perfectly on the green.
The chip shot helped him save par.
She practiced her chip shots for better accuracy.
The tricky chip shot required precision.

sense.definition: a long, powerful golf shot from the tee

examples.x: His drive went straight down the fairway.
She has a powerful drive that impresses everyone.
The drive set him up for an easy approach shot.
The drive landed in the middle of the fairway.
He practiced his drive to increase distance.

sense.definition: the action of hitting the golf ball with the club

examples.x: His swing is smooth and controlled.
She practiced her swing for hours.
He adjusted his swing to improve accuracy.
His swing speed increased with training.
He perfected his swing at the driving range.

sense.definition: (golf) a score of one stroke less than the par value for a hole, typically achieved by sinking the ball into the hole with one fewer stroke than the expected number

examples.x: He sank a birdie putt from six feet away.
The golfer celebrated after making a birdie on the par-3 hole.
He chipped in for birdie from the edge of the green.
The golfer's accurate iron shot set up a birdie chance.
They marked their birdie on the scorecard with a red circle.

sense.definition: a score of one stroke over par on a hole in golf

examples.x: He made a bogey on the third hole.
She was disappointed with her bogey on the final hole.
She was frustrated with her bogey on the easy par 3.
He finished the round with a bogey on the 18th hole.

sense.definition: a score of two strokes under par on a hole in golf

examples.x: She carded an eagle with a brilliant approach shot.
She made an eagle by hitting a perfect second shot.
He scored an eagle on the par 5 by sinking a long putt.
She celebrated her eagle on the challenging par 4.
She recorded an eagle and two birdies in her round.

sense.definition: (golf) the remarkable achievement of sinking the ball into the cup with just one stroke on a par-3 hole

examples.x: Alex's skillful putt ended with a hole in one.
Mike's ball landed straight into the cup, clinching a hole in one.
A hole in one was achieved by Jake's remarkable stroke.
Lily's fantastic shot earned her a hole in one.
Ben's shot, finding its target perfectly, secured a hole in one.

sense.definition: the standard number of strokes expected for a skilled golfer to complete a hole or course

examples.x: He made par on every hole to finish even for the round.
She needs a par on the last hole to win the tournament.
His consistent play led to several pars in a row.
She missed the green but managed to save par with a great chip.
He hit a long putt to secure par on the challenging par 3.

sense.definition: a golf competition format where players compete hole by hole, aiming to win individual holes rather than building up a total score

examples.x: He prefers match play over stroke play because it's more competitive.
She won the match play tournament by defeating her opponent on the final hole.
She lost in the semi-finals of the match play event after a close contest.
He enjoys the intensity of match play where every hole matters.
His strategy in match play is to focus on winning holes rather than playing conservatively.

sense.definition: a numerical measure of a golf player's ability, used to level the playing field in competition

examples.x: Players with a lower handicap are expected to perform better in tournaments.
It's challenging to compete against someone with a much lower handicap.
The handicap system allows golfers of different skill levels to compete fairly.
Even with a high handicap, he manages to enjoy the game every time he plays.
He's working hard to lower his handicap before the big tournament.

sense.definition: the movement of the golf club from the top of the swing down to the point of impact with the ball

examples.x: His downswing was smooth and controlled, resulting in a powerful shot.
She struggled with her timing on the downswing during the round.
His downswing was too steep, causing him to hit behind the ball.

sense.definition: a golf shot that curves sharply to the left for right-handed golfers or to the right for left-handed golfers

examples.x: He hit a hook that curved around the trees and landed on the fairway.
His hook got worse as the round progressed, affecting his score.
His hook became predictable under pressure, leading to bogeys.
She practiced hitting straighter shots to eliminate her hook.

sense.definition: a piece of turf or grass that is displaced when a player strikes the ground with their club during a swing

examples.x: The divot from her tee shot left a noticeable mark on the fairway.
They filled in divots on the practice range to maintain the turf.
His divot pattern showed inconsistency in his swing technique.
She learned to take divots after improving her ball-striking skills.
His divot flew further than his ball on a poorly executed shot.

sense.definition: the positioning a golfer takes before making a swing to address the ball

examples.x: Her coach emphasized a proper address to improve her consistency.
They practiced their address routine to set up for accurate shots.
His address at the ball showed confidence and focus before each swing.
She struggled with her address, leading to inconsistent ball striking.
His address included a waggle to relax his hands before starting his swing.

sense.definition: the distance a golf ball travels through the air from the point of impact to where it first hits the ground

examples.x: You need to factor in the wind for the ball's carry.
The ball's carry was affected by the uphill slope.
Her tee shot had a perfect carry onto the green.
The ball's carry was shorter than expected due to the cold weather.
A strong tailwind helped increase the ball's carry.

sense.definition: a stroke played toward the green from a location farther away

examples.x: His approach shot landed on the green just a few feet from the pin.
She practiced her approach shots from various distances to improve accuracy.
The approach shot required careful consideration of wind direction and green slope.
He chipped in for birdie after a precise approach shot from the fairway.
Her approach shot rolled past the hole but left an easy putt for par.

sense.definition: the playing period for each part of the course

examples.x: They completed the first hole in under par.
He struggled on the back nine, averaging 4.5 strokes per hole.
The 9th hole was particularly challenging with a tight fairway and deep bunkers.
She had a consistent round, never taking more than three putts on any hole.
He eagled the par-5 15th hole with a long approach shot and a perfect putt.

sense.definition: a card used in golf to record each player's scores for each hole during a round

examples.x: Please hand me the scorecard so I can write down our scores.
My scorecard got wet in the rain, and now the ink is running.
He recorded a birdie on the 7th hole on his scorecard.
The scorecard shows his handicap as a 12.
His scorecard indicated he had played the front nine in 39 strokes.

sense.definition: a type of shot where the ball curves unintentionally to the right for right-handed golfers or to the left for left-handed golfers in flight

examples.x: He struggled with his slice, often sending the ball into the rough.
She adjusted her grip to minimize the slice on her drives.
His slice started straight but curved sharply to the right midway.
He analyzed his swing to identify the cause of his slice.
The slice caused the ball to veer off course and land in the bunker.

sense.definition: a golfer who achieves the lowest score in a medal play competition, earning them the title of medalist for that tournament

examples.x: The golfer became the medalist by shooting the lowest score of the day.
The young golfer aspired to be the medalist in the upcoming championship.
During the tournament, the medalist demonstrated exceptional skill and consistency.
Every golfer aims to be the medalist by playing their best round of golf.
She worked on refining her short game to improve her chances of becoming a medalist.

sense.definition: of a golfer who is capable of playing to the standard of the course rating
to gently hit the golf ball towards the hole on the green

examples.x: He putt the ball to within inches of the hole.
She carefully putt to avoid overshooting.
He putt with a smooth, steady stroke.
She needs to putt better to improve her score.

sense.definition: (golf) to play a hole in one stroke, usually referring to a hole-in-one where the ball is hit from the tee into the cup

examples.x: She hoped to ace the tricky 15th hole to secure her first career hole-in-one.
He aced the par-3 7th hole with a perfect shot that landed directly in the hole.
The golfer aced the short par-3, eliciting cheers from the gallery.
He was thrilled to ace the hole, marking a memorable moment in his golfing career.
She aced the hole-in-one challenge during the tournament, winning a new car.

sense.definition: to strike the ball with the heel of the golf club

examples.x: The amateur player often heels his putts under pressure.
The golfer heeled his approach shot, leaving it short of the green.
She heeled the ball into the bunker instead of reaching the green.
She tends to heel her shots when she gets nervous.
He accidentally heeled his drive off the tee, causing it to slice into the rough.

sense.definition: (in golf) to make a short, high-arching shot, typically played from closer distances to the hole

examples.x: He pitched the ball onto the green, aiming to land it softly near the pin.
She pitched her approach shot to the elevated green, trying to clear the bunker.
The golfer pitched the ball over the water hazard onto the green with precision.
The golfer decided to pitch the ball instead of using a full swing due to the tight lie.

sense.definition: to successfully hit the ball into the hole with a putt, finishing the hole

examples.x: They waited anxiously as the golfer prepared to hole out for the win.
They celebrated loudly when he holed out for birdie on the final green.
She was relieved to hole out after a challenging round of golf.
He managed to hole out in two shots on the par-4 hole.
